hypothesis: The LLM's tool-selection strategy in MatAgent's Planning stage will exhibit measurable transferability across target properties such that a policy trained (via reinforcement learning on tool-selection sequences) to optimize formation energy will, without retraining, accelerate convergence toward distinct target properties—such as band gap or bulk modulus—more rapidly than the default prompted tool-selection behavior, because the temporal patterns of tool switching (e.g., knowledge base early, long-term memory late) encode generalizable meta-strategies for navigating compositional space that are property-agnostic rather than specific to formation energy optimization.
